Ad Text:
About Bower Insights

Bower Insights is a forward-thinking consultancy specialising in property advisory, development, economics, policy, and research.

We exist to bridge the gap between policy intent and delivery, helping organisations turn vision into impact. We believe in the power of informed decisions, and in the people behind them, to create lasting change. Our purpose is to provide insights and services that enable our clients to achieve positive outcomes.

About the role

Bower Insights is seeking an experienced Lead Economist to lead our economics service stream and support the delivery of a range of client projects. The Lead Economist will be the Practice Lead for our economics service stream, responsible for the management and growth of the service stream, including responsibility for service stream P&L.

We’re looking for people with the right personal qualities and mindset, supported by an appropriate skill set. The position is suited to experienced professionals with experience in economic analysis and development, policy and research.

Key responsibilities

Lead Bower Insights’ economic service stream

Lead client projects within the economics service stream

Conduct socio-economic and cost-benefit analysis

Financial modelling, forecasting and scenario modelling, including housing and infrastructure modelling

Policy evaluation and analysis

Deliver high-quality market research and economic/property analysis across a diverse range of projects

Work with economic, financial or other data to generate unique and actionable insights

Build positive client relationships to drive the business development and growth of the economics service stream

Liaise with clients to understand their needs and deliver tailored solutions

Co-ordinate and communicate effectively with internal and market facing stakeholders including clients, consultants and contractors

Prepare regular status reports and communicate updates to stakeholders

Support and mentorship of junior team members

Responsibility for the economics service stream P&L

Collaborate with other service streams to support organisational business growth

 

About you

Formal qualification in quantitative or economics-based discipline preferred

Demonstrated minimum 8 years’ experience in relevant consultancy roles, ideally within the property, government or economics sector

Demonstrated experience in leading business development activities and being accountable for service or team P&L preferred

Strong understanding of urban economics and housing policy

Government advisory experience preferred

Excellent research, analysis and problem-solving skills

Strong organisational and administration skills

Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, with expertise in other analytical tools desirable.

High level of emotional intelligence, interpersonal and leadership skills

Strong commercial acumen with the ability to manage contracts, budgets, and deliverables independently

Capacity to manage competing priorities and meet strict deadlines




How to apply 

Please provide your CV and a cover letter addressing how you meet the requirements of this role through Seek.




Why Bower Insights?

At Bower Insights, we believe in creating a workplace where people thrive. Our culture is built on collaboration, innovation, and respect. We value diversity and encourage fresh ideas that help us deliver meaningful insights to our clients.

We are a people-focused business, and supporting our staff to do work they enjoy and grow professionally is a core component of our approach.




What we offer

A supportive and inclusive team environment

Opportunities for professional growth and development

Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance

Competitive salary and bonus package




We are an Equal Opportunity Employer

At Bower Insights, we celebrate diversity and value the unique perspectives that each person in our team brings. We believe that different experiences and ideas spark innovation, strengthen our services, and create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. First Nations people, those identifying as LGBTQIA+, women, people of all ages, people with disabilities and culturally and linguistically diverse people are encouraged to apply.

The Opportunity:
 
This is a rare find, a high-impact Senior Analyst role that puts you at the centre of executive decision-making, without the burnout that tends to come with it.
We're a small, listed company, small enough that your work is visible and will give you exposure to the CEO and the ELT, and large enough that the problems are genuinely complex and consequential. There's no bureaucratic filter between your analysis and the people acting on it.
The role exists to serve as a strategic and analytical force multiplier for our GM Supply Chain, extending their reach across strategy, commercial, procurement and operational topics, and ensuring that every significant decision is grounded in rigorous, clear-eyed insight.
 
What You'll Actually Do:
 
You'll be the analytical engine behind some of our most important decisions. That means synthesising messy, ambiguous inputs into crisp executive conclusions; building business cases and scenario models that hold up under scrutiny; assessing markets and opportunities with the same rigour you'd apply in consulting but with the satisfaction of seeing your work directly shape outcomes.
 
Specifically, you'll:

Act as a trusted analytical partner to the GM Supply Chain and senior leaders across operations strategy, commercials, and procurement.
Prepare and quality-assure executive papers, owning the narrative, the assumptions, and the decision-readiness.
Conduct market and opportunity assessments, including TAM/SAM/SOM, competitive dynamics and strategic fit.
Analyse operational economics, including contribution, margin, breakeven, total cost of ownership, and translate them into clear investment and capability decisions.
Lead and support strategic procurement initiatives.
Model business outcomes across scenarios and provide the upside/downside perspectives executives need to make risk-adjusted calls.
Keep transformation initiatives honest, tracking milestones, benefits and risks, and providing independent challenge where things drift.
Who We're Looking For:
 
You've ideally come from a Big 4 or top-tier consultancy, or from a corporate finance, strategy or in-house analytics function where the work was demanding and the standards were high.
 
You don't need to tick every box. What matters most is that you're:

Highly analytical and commercially sharp so you can move fluidly between big-picture strategy and granular financial modelling.
A structured thinker, you can take an ambiguous problem, frame it clearly, and turn it into something decision-ready.
A strong communicator; your written and verbal output is clean, confident and calibrated for executive audiences.
Comfortable challenging up so you'll push back when assumptions don't stack up, and you'll do it with evidence.
A hands-on executor, you simplify complexity without losing the substance, and you follow through.
Formally, we'd expect a business-related degree, around seven or more years of relevant experience, and demonstrated capability across strategy, commercial modelling and financial analysis. Exposure to procurement or operational transformation is a plus.
 
Why This Role:

Direct access working closely with the GM Supply Chain and have genuine visibility to the CEO and broader ELT. Your work won't disappear into a slide deck; it will drive decisions.
Scope without the grind, the problems are meaty and varied, but this is a senior individual contributor role with no people or P&L responsibility. You own your work, not a team.
Flexibility - we know that the right person for this role may be weighing lifestyle considerations. We're open to conversations about how the role is structured, and we'll trade on flexibility for the right fit.
Scale that lets you matter; as a listed company of our size, there's no hiding behind layers of hierarchy. What you produce has a direct line to business outcomes.
If you're a partial match but the shape of this role appeals, we'd still encourage a conversation.
 
Benefits & Culture:

GALE Safe - Focused safety culture and practices
Supportive leadership and connected team environment
Opportunities for professional learning and development
Braeside location with modern facilities and onsite parking
Flexible working supported with WFH days
Paid Parental Leave Program
Access to comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Wellbeing support
Novated Leasing
Staff product discounts
 
About GALE Pacific:
 
GALE Pacific celebrates our 75th anniversary in 2026. Our business progressed from an entrepreneurial family business knitting scarves and baby clothes to pioneering the development of knitting HDPE into what we know as shade fabric today.    We are a category-leading provider of innovative shade solutions, transforming how people experience and enjoy outdoor spaces. Specialising in high-quality, sustainable products, from umbrellas and sun sails to awnings and custom shade structures, we design for performance, protection, and style in the world's harshest climates. Our range is endorsed by the Cancer Council, a partnership created through a shared commitment to education and innovation in sun protection.    Our vision is to make shade as fundamental to outdoor life as sunlight — where every person, animal, crop and shared space is protected from the sun's harsh effects, so communities thrive through sustainable, intelligent and accessible shade.    GALE Pacific is a public company (ASX: GAP) and employs over 400 people around the globe, operating in Australia, China, the United States, Europe and the Middle East. The opportunities for growth are endless. GALE Pacific is an equal opportunity employer. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need, not personal or protected attributes. We welcome applications from all cultural backgrounds, abilities and experiences. Please advise our team if you require assistance throughout the recruitment process.    As part of our recruitment process, candidates may be asked to complete online psychometric testing, medical assessments (including Alcohol & Other Drug) and onsite / virtual interviews

Are you a data-driven professional with a knack for turning complex information into strategic gold? We are looking for an Insights Officer (AO7) to join a dynamic team in Brisbane CBD on an initial contract until 30 June 2026. In this role, you will bridge the gap between raw data and actionable advice, utilising both traditional research and AI-powered tools to support initiatives. If you enjoy high-level stakeholder engagement and creating impactful reports that influence state and national policy, this role is for you.

What You Will Do:
Conduct complex research and data analysis on trade, economic, and policy issues at state, national, and global levels.
Develop strategic insights using both human-led and Artificial Intelligence powered sources to provide pragmatic advice to diverse audiences.
Produce impactful products and high-quality briefings, reports, and presentations for internal and external stakeholders.
Perform qualitative and quantitative research to identify data-driven tactical opportunities and support operational teams.
Manage ad hoc data requests to identify specific strategic insights that drive client engagement and trade outcomes.
What You Need to Apply for This Role:
Proven expertise in data analysis and the ability to formulate meaningful advice from multiple complex information sources.
Strong stakeholder management skills to build trusted networks across private and public sector organisations.
Experience in communication strategies and negotiation, with the ability to liaise effectively across different cultures.
Ability to develop work programs and standardised tools for critical thinking and analytical approaches.
Strategic relationship building skills to foster commitment toward long-term growth and overcoming investment and migration challenges

And all Randstad Technologies contractors receive:

Access to a Udemy license for ongoing learning and continuous professional development
Access to exclusive discounts across various platforms
Discounts on private health funds

For more information, or to apply for this role, please contact Jake on jake.kennedy@randstaddigital.com.au

At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

About Us

Globally, energy markets are in transition to a net zero future and the entities within these markets are transforming their underlying business models to ensure that they stay relevant, sustainable and profitable. As one of Australia's largest energy providers, we are proud of our purpose to lead and accelerate the clean energy transformation. Our ability to deliver on this purpose will be dependent on our people.

As the energy sector rapidly evolves with electrification, renewables, and sustainability at the forefront, we are delivering innovative centralised energy solutions to meet the needs of modern communities.

About this Role

As the Data Steward, you will be embedded within a business domain and accountable for ensuring the quality, consistency, and integrity of key data assets. You will act as the bridge between business and technology teams, championing data governance standards, resolving data quality issues, and helping to build a strong, data‑driven culture across the organisation.

This will include:

Acting as the primary point of accountability for data governance and data quality within your assigned business domain
Building strong relationships with data owners, subject matter experts, and stakeholders to drive adoption of enterprise data governance standards
Developing and maintaining data quality scorecards, identifying risks and issues, and working collaboratively to resolve them
Identifying and documenting critical data elements, contributing to the enterprise business glossary, and ensuring accurate metadata and data lineage
Supporting data ownership and stewardship frameworks, including onboarding data owners and delivering training to uplift data literacy
Driving compliance with information governance requirements such as data classification, retention, privacy, and PII handling

About You

You are a collaborative and detail‑oriented data professional who is passionate about improving data quality and trust. You enjoy working across business and technology teams, can influence without authority, and take a structured, pragmatic approach to problem solving. You are comfortable operating in complex environments and motivated by enabling better decisions through better data.

You will bring:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Business, Information Management, or equivalent practical experience.
Experience in data governance, data stewardship, data quality, or master data management
A strong understanding of data governance concepts such as metadata management, business glossaries, data lineage, and data classification
Proven ability to engage and influence stakeholders to drive behavioural and process change
Experience using data governance, data catalogue, or data observability tools (e.g. Alation, Monte Carlo, or similar)
The ability to identify data risks and issues and drive resolution through clear, structured processes

How to Apply

If you share our passion for a clean energy future for all Australians and want to join us #Doing. Not just dreaming, then click the 'APPLY' button.

Applications close 3rd April 2026

Why Us?

EnergyAustralia is a place where all employees are genuinely valued, supported, and free to be themselves — because we believe that's when we're at our best.

We embrace diversity of background, experience, and perspective, and we encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability, and those from the LGBTQ+ community.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

About the role 

You will join the Role Insights team within Hirer Insights at SEEK, playing a pivotal role in transforming marketplace and candidate interaction data into clear, self-service insights that help hirers understand market conditions and make confident, data-driven decisions. This role is dedicated to optimizing the existing experience and exploring the desirability and viability of new insight features across supply and demand, salary, candidate location, and competitor activity.

You will collaborate closely with product managers, data analysts, engineers, and go-to-market stakeholders to balance high-quality user experiences with diverse business and product objectives. Working within cross-functional squads, you will create innovative solutions through your expertise in user research, interaction design, visual design, and user validation methods while solving design problems at scale across multiple Asia-Pacific markets.

The team operates with a strong agile mindset, embracing rapid experimentation and frequent feature releases, offering a start-up feel backed by SEEK's scale and support. You will own and help drive best-practice experiences across SEEK's insights ecosystem, advocate for users in business decisions, and influence design priorities for continuous uplift while ensuring products continue to set industry standards for clarity, usability, and decision support.

What you'll do

Plan and scope design initiatives aligned with product outcomes and timelines, balancing progress over perfection in experimental contexts.
Shape product direction and roadmap by understanding customer needs, platform capabilities, and industry trends in recruitment and user experience.
Lead end-to-end design efforts including research, workshopping, prototyping, usability testing, and data analysis for complex challenges.
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to solve problems, balance trade-offs, and deliver impactful solutions.
Communicate and defend design decisions through compelling storytelling, ensuring alignment with company objectives and user insights.
Leverage and evolve the Design System for consistency, creating bespoke components and interaction patterns when necessary.
Design inclusive solutions tailored to the cultural and linguistic diversity of Asia-Pacific audiences.
Investigate emerging technologies including AI and personalization to enhance platform functionality and user experience with advanced AI tool usage.
Qualifications

Essential skills and experience 

5+ years of experience in online or software product design, ideally at marketplace or SaaS company with significant senior-level experience.
Strong user research capabilities with proficiency in planning, conducting research, usability testing, and generating actionable insight reports.
Ability to define design plans balancing high-quality outputs within fast timeframes, exercising strong judgment on when to go deep versus lean.
Ability to context-switch effectively across concurrent tasks and rapidly process complex design, research, and stakeholder requirements and input.
Proven ability to transform customer insights into beautiful, effortless experiences with expertise in polished UI and accessibility standards.
Skilled in leading design thinking techniques, especially workshop facilitation for scope guidance and fast ideation in agile environments.
Advanced proficiency in Figma with strong verbal and written communication skills to articulate design decisions aligned with business goals.
Advanced usage of AI tools across design process stages to accelerate outputs, insights, and deliverables with comfort in fast-paced iteration.

Desirable skills and experience

Familiarity with research and analytics tools like HotJar, Intercom, and Dovetail or similar.
Knowledge of collaboration tools such as Jira, Confluence, and Miro.
Ability to align design decisions with both user and business goals.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, iterative environment with shifting priorities.
Desire to work in a start-up like environment, open to try and learn things in a fast-paced way of working.
Additional Information

Perks and benefits
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Free income protection insurance
Frequent events including sports days, annual Christmas party, hackathon, and trivia

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Job Title: Multiple Maximo Consultants - Solution Lead, Project Manager and Business Analys
Ad Text:
Multiple Maximo Consultants – Solution Lead, Project Manager and Business Analyst

6 month contracts
Location: Melbourne (preferred), Adelaide (second option)
Rates from $700 – 1200 p/d depending on the position
Project Overview:

This program involves the large-scale migration and redeployment of IBM Maximo to support asset and maintenance operations across the organisation
The initiative is focused on modernising existing systems, enhancing integration capabilities (including GIS, BizTalk/AIS and Historian platforms), and improving reporting through BIRT
Delivery will follow a phased, wave-based approach to ensure minimal disruption to ongoing operations and maintain business continuity
A key component of the program includes complex data migration, integration readiness, and alignment to the target operating model
The project requires close collaboration across business, technology, infrastructure and third-party vendors to ensure successful delivery and transition to BAU operations
Roles Available:
Our client are seeking 3 positions for a Project Manager, Solution Lead and a Business Analyst
Project Manager

Lead end-to-end delivery across planning, execution, cutover and transition to BAU
Manage governance, RAID, financials, scope and key delivery milestones
Coordinate across internal teams and third-party vendors to manage dependencies
Drive cutover planning and ensure operational readiness for go-live
Provide clear reporting, stakeholder comms and escalation management
Maximo Lead

Own Maximo solution delivery including configuration, integrations and reporting
Provide technical leadership across design, build and deployment phases
Ensure alignment between business requirements and Maximo capabilities
Oversee integration readiness across GIS, Historian and middleware platforms
Support testing, defect resolution and technical cutover
Business Analyst

Gather and document business, operational and reporting requirements
Map current and future state processes aligned to Maximo implementation
Define and validate integration and data migration requirements
Support testing including UAT, defect triage and business sign-off
Contribute to documentation, runbooks and business readiness for go-live

SYSTRA is a global engineering consultancy that provides services across transportation, technology, and security sectors. With more than 11000 professional employees, our engineering capabilities ensure we deliver world-class, safe and sustainable solutions to projects around the world.

When you join SYSTRA you can expect to be part of a connected, passionate team that are motivated to find innovative solutions. SYSTRA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace that values difference. We provide a collaborative environment, supporting our employees to achieve career goals and maximise potential.

The Opportunity:

A career at SYSTRA provides the opportunity for you to apply your Human Factors expertise across a variety of high-profile projects. Reporting to Director - Human Factors, based in Sydney or Brisbane, the position will be responsible for the provision of Human Factors subject matter expertise and consulting services, working closely with clients across the ANZ transport sector. This will allow you to work autonomously, with support from the wider Human Factors team or as part of a multi-disciplinary SYSTRA team depending on the project. The role will be part of a well-established, collaborative and respected team comprising HF specialists across Australia and the UK.


Your new role:


Provide consulting services to clients in the field of Human Factors, predominantly in rail.
Lead the technical delivery of HF work on transport projects and undertake Human Factors Integration and Assurance activities.
Cross-collaborate with other team members to minimise project risks and enhance the positive integration/delivery of SYSTRA services.
Be a trusted contributor to tender submissions, leveraging your knowledge from a Human Factors perspective.
Provide Human Factors support in other sectors as opportunities arise.
Collaborate with other SYSTRA teams to scope and deliver an integrated service offering, particularly within SE/SA and Operational Integration.
Provide mentoring under the SYSTRA HF graduate program and support HF graduate and consultant development opportunities.
Promote the SYSTRA brand to help the team secure new business opportunities within new and existing projects.




Required experience & skills:


Degree Qualified in Human Factors, Ergonomics, Psychology or a related field.
A minimum of 8 years' experience in the application of HF methods, with previous Human Factors experience in the rail sector.
Experience in Human Factors Integration and Assurance, the application of established methods and a working knowledge of applicable standards.
Able to perform detailed research and analysis coupled with proposal writing.
Proven abilities to build sound working relationships across diverse groups, internally and externally.
Demonstrate a robust commercial acumen and a proactive approach to assist in the development of innovative solutions.



Benefits of working at SYSTRA:


At SYSTRA, we prioritise the well-being and professional growth of our employees by offering a comprehensive range of benefits. We're committed to nurturing your career development, providing ongoing support for obtaining Chartership status and providing access to continuous learning opportunities. Furthermore, we cover the costs of professional memberships and subscriptions, ensuring you stay connected to industry developments.

Recognising the importance of maintaining a healthy work-life balance, we offer flexible working arrangements and generous paid parental leave. Our inclusive workplace culture, endorsed by Work180, celebrates diversity and values every individual's unique contribution. To further demonstrate our appreciation for our team members, we offer attractive employee referral incentives as a token of recognition for their valuable contributions.

Click this link to view all our benefits: https://work180.com/en-au/for-women/employer/systra

At SYSTRA ANZ, we value diversity of thought and experience. We encourage applications from Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders and people from diverse backgrounds.

Our values are centered around Connected Teams, Excellence and Bold Leadership

Job Title: Practice Manager - CX and Conversational AI
Salary: Practice Manager - CX and Conversational AI
Ad Text:
Our Why 

Datacom works with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to make a difference in people’s lives and help organisations use the power of tech to innovate and grow.

About the Role (Your Why) 

At Datacom, we connect people and technology to solve challenges, create opportunities, and unlock new possibilities for the communities we serve. Our mission is simple: make a meaningful difference through the power of innovation. We partner with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to improve lives and help businesses use technology to innovate, grow, and succeed.

As a Conversational AI & CX leader you will blend hands-on technical expertise with commercial acumen and the ability to engage deeply with customers at every level. You’ll design, build, showcase and optimise cutting-edge conversational solutions that inspire and deliver measurable business value, driving improved customer and employee experiences.

If you’re passionate about customers and obsessed with designing human-centric experiences that deliver ROI, this is your stage. You see the bigger picture, yet dive into the details, and you’re recognised as a thought leader and practitioner in the CX and EX space.

About the team

You’ll be part of our dynamic, fast-growing Conversational AI team across ANZ and Asia, collaborating with some of the most innovative and recognisable brands in Australia and New Zealand. In this pivotal role, you’ll help shape next-generation service experiences by applying Conversational AI and broader AI capabilities to transform customer engagement. Your ability to navigate ambiguity and guide teams through technical and organisational complexity will be essential to driving impactful outcomes across a broad range of industries.

We value flexibility – our offices are based in Adelaide, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, Perth, Sydney, – and we support working from home too. We empower you and your team to balance collaboration and work-life harmony. If you’re an innovative thinker who thrives at the intersection of technology and customer outcomes, you’ll inspire clients with visionary ideas while delivering tangible, high-quality solutions. Your collaborative approach, technical credibility, and passion for CX/EX will make you a trusted advisor and a driver of change.

What You’ll Do

We work in an agile environment where no two days are the same – responding to evolving customer, technology, and business needs. You will:

Create compelling, customer-focused experiences powered by Conversational AI technologies and be comfortable presenting and discussing these with customers and prospects
Ideate, design, build, and deliver powerful demonstrations that showcase CAI capabilities to existing and prospective clients
Apply advanced conversational design principles to craft intuitive, human-centric interaction flows
Transition seamlessly between deep technical conversations with engineers and strategic discussions with business leaders
Collaborate with customers to shape their CX and EX visions – inspiring new ideas and provoking thoughts about the ‘art of the possible’
Identify automation opportunities in contact centre and retail environments that deliver ROI while enhancing frontline operations
Manage and optimise delivery of current solutions including intent management, conversational structure and integration with customers' digital environments
Work with customers to identify tuning and refining opportunities to increase bot containment and improve agent and customer satisfaction within risk tolerance and accessibility standards
Work effectively across stakeholders to translate ambitious customer visions into practical, scalable solutions
Apply change management methodologies and agile delivery techniques to ensure seamless adoption and lasting impact
Plan and integrate next‑generation technologies into the architectural strategy and build strategic relationships with software and hardware vendors to inform roadmaps and GTM approaches

What you’ll bring

Required Experience

Proven (3+ years) expertise in Conversational AI and CX strategy delivering measurable improvements
Strong understanding of automation in contact centre and retail environments
Ability to lead and contribute to project management and implementation activities
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and communication skills, able to navigate technical and business conversations
Experience with platforms such as Twilio, Cognigy, Genesys, and Microsoft
Proven leadership experience and excellent presentation and stakeholder management skills
Experience in solution and design services, scoping, configuration assistance and needs assessments

Nice to Have

Strong working knowledge of change management and agile delivery frameworks
Background in outsourcing or working with systems integrators
Experience with Prosci Change Management methodology
Familiarity with ITIL, application management and integration into core business systems
Understanding of key cloud vendors such as AWS, Azure, GCP and commercial awareness of vendor architectural strategies

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services, while maintaining a dynamic, agile, small-business feel. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy.

At Datacom you'll be recognised and valued for your contributions. We're growing year on year and can provide stability, career opportunity and a collegial, agile, flat-structured environment that empowers people and promotes autonomy. We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. We operate at the leading edge of technology to help ANZ’s largest enterprise organisations explore possibilities and solve their greatest challenges, so you will never run out of interesting new challenges and opportunities.

If you would like to know more and this sounds like you. Please apply online today!

We are seeking a highly strategic Enterprise Architect to lead the development of an Enterprise Technology Strategy and supporting domain roadmaps. This critical engagement involves conducting comprehensive business capability assessments and authoring executive-ready strategies that define the organisations technology direction and investment priorities for the next 3-5 years. If you are a visionary leader who excels at bridging the gap between business requirements and technical execution within complex, regulated environments, this is the ideal opportunity to shape a large-scale digital landscape.

What You Will Do:

Assess business capability requirements and maturity to develop a comprehensive application capability mapping and gap analysis.
Author an overarching Enterprise Technology Strategy and a specific SAP Strategy and Roadmap aligned to business investment priorities.
Develop domain-level architecture strategies across Digital & Technology, Cloud & Infrastructure, and Application Rationalisation.
Translate identified capability gaps into sequenced investment themes, initiatives, and multi-year transition roadmaps.
Lead executive-level stakeholder engagement and workshop facilitation to ensure alignment across IT and business leadership.

What You Need to Apply for This Role:

Proven experience leading multi-domain enterprise architecture engagements including applications, integration, infrastructure, and data.
Demonstrated expertise in authoring executive-ready strategy artefacts, principles, standards, and target state models.
Strong capability in business capability modelling and aligning technology roadmaps to pragmatic business outcomes.
Solid understanding of application rationalisation, cloud migration patterns, and integration principles.
Exceptional communication skills with the ability to produce concise, structured documentation suitable for GM and ELT consumption.

Interested? Apply Now!

If you are an Enterprise Architect with experience in SAP strategy and road mapping, we want to hear from you. Click "Apply Now".

Alternatively, please reach out to Josh for more information - joshua.sharp@randstaddigital.com.au

Randstad is searching for a skilled and accomplished Senior Policy Advisor who possesses a keen eye for detail and excels in their field. This role requires the ability to build and sustain strong relationships. Our collaborative department is in need of an enthusiastic individual who embraces challenges and is committed to making a positive impact within education.

About your new role

The Senior Policy Adviser is a high-level subject-matter expert responsible for providing leading governance and corporate law expertise within a multi-disciplinary education and policy leadership department. The position demands a self-starter with a commitment to excellence, highly developed written skills, and the personal leadership necessary to influence both internal and external stakeholders.

What you will need to exceed

Deep knowledge of corporate governance practice and regulation.

Highly developed drafting skills to produce policy submissions, research reports, and presentations.

Analytical prowess to evaluate complex policy options and provide recommendations for reform.

Strong networking abilities to build deep professional connections and relate effectively to a community of leaders.

Resilience to cope with pressure and setbacks while maintaining high professional standards.

Direct experience providing advice to boards and directors on governance matters.

Proven experience in public policy advocacy and government relations.

Sector Relevance: Experience within Federal Government is highly preferred. We are looking for candidates with a background in Treasury, Cyber, Digital, Corporations Act, Business, or Regulatory environments. Experience within the Attorney General's office is also highly regarded.

Must have

A degree in Law, Politics, Commerce, or Economics (candidates with extensive relevant experience may be considered if they do not hold a law degree).

Experience specifically with a Federal regulator, government body, or investor group.

Next steps

If you are a policy expert with a commitment to inclusivity, collaboration, and transformative change, we invite you to apply and be part of this inspiring journey.

This role suits an experienced analytics professional ready to operate at a strategic level, governing platforms, influencing architecture decisions, and embedding data quality across a large, complex organisation. Advanced Power BI skills are essential, with a background in resources, energy, or oil and gas highly regarded.

Continually improve analytics and data services delivered to the business.

Profile

Post-secondary qualification in Information Management, Information Systems, or equivalent.
Minimum 10+ years' experience in analytics and data.
Advanced skills in Microsoft Power BI (essential), Microsoft Fabric (desirable), and SAP Datasphere (desirable).
Oil and gas experience and/or consulting/professional services background is desirable.
A track record of delivering analytics and data solutions within complex organisations and the resources industry, with oil and gas experience highly desirable.
Ability to assess and analyse complex analytics and data situations and develop compelling propositions.
Experience in IT governance.
Experience in analytics technical architecture, data architecture, solution design, and implementation.
Expertise across a combination of leading and emerging analytics and data technologies.
A track record of developing and maintaining positive stakeholder relationships across all levels of management, including executive level.
Excellent presentation and communication skills for effective stakeholder and staff engagement.
A passion for working in high-performance teams.
Effective team player with strong attention to detail.
Commitment to excellence and continuous improvement in the quality of work produced.

Ad Text:
About Central Steel Build


In 1975, Central Steel Build began as a small family business with a humble, customer focused vision: to purpose build high quality sheds which would stand the test of time. We're now well into our second generation, and while our design and fabrication methods have become more sophisticated with cutting-edge modelling technology added to the mix, we've stayed true to our ideology of continuous improvement.

Our values shape us

Creating client value – we have a 'customer first' approach, driven by innovation, speed and attention to detail.

Working together – we demonstrate team energy by emulating a trusting, collaborative and results-focused workplace.

Continuous improvement – we see change as a smart opportunity to remain nimble, adaptable and accountable.

Being respectful – we foster a positive and humble mindset that listens and encourages.

Openly communicating – we have honest and fun two-way communication that is constructive and responsive.

Fast-Growing Manufacturing Business

Central Steel Build is embarking on a major AI, automation, and systems transformation. With a turnover of ~$65M and strong growth ambitions, we are implementing a full company-wide AI and automation roadmap.

 We are now searching for a strategic and hands-on technology leader who can own the architecture, build the data foundations, and lead our evolution into a modern, data-driven manufacturing organisation.

Reporting to the CEO, this role has enterprise‑wide accountability for architecture, data platforms, system integration, analytics, and AI governance.

The Role

The Head of Technology & Data is a strategic and hands‑on leader responsible for:

Designing and owning the enterprise architecture.

Establishing CSB’s AI operating model and governance.

Leading all system integration, automation, and data strategy.

Building and maintaining the Unified Analytics Layer.

Ensuring CSB has clean, reliable, real-time data to power AI agents, automated workflows, reporting, and future innovation.

Acting as the key executive partner to our external AI consultants while embedding capability inside CSB.

This is a role for a CTO-level leader who can think strategically AND tactically execute. 

This is a lead-by-doing and lead-by-thinking role. While you are not expected to build all solutions personally, you must be deeply comfortable with the detail – able to engage credibly with infrastructure architects, software engineers, data specialists, and vendors, and make informed decisions that balance technical excellence with business value.

About You

You will thrive in this role if you have:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field.

8+ years of experience across technology leadership, enterprise architecture, or data engineering

Experience integrating or architecting around NetSuite or a similar ERP

Strong hands-on capability in Microsoft ecosystems, SQL, Azure, ETL/ELT pipelines, and Power BI.

Understanding of APIs, integrations, and workflow automation

A passion for agentic AI, automation, and improving how people work.

Experience in manufacturing, construction, fabrication, or supply chain environments (preferred)

You enjoy both long-term strategy and rolling up your sleeves to build.

 You exhibit professional traits, including:

Exhibiting excellent judgment and high standards

Thinking strategically, but staying on top of tactical execution

Thinking big and standing firm in your convictions

Being results-oriented

Developing great people and being able to inspire passion in others

 Why Join Us?

Exciting opportunity: drive transformation for a growing business delivering superior results.

Great team culture: Join a positive, collaborative work environment that is strongly values-driven.

State-of-the-art facilities: Work in a modern, cutting-edge building alongside a skilled, multidisciplinary team.

Lead data, integration and automation at the centre of Banyule's transformation journey.

Banyule City Council is undertaking a significant transformation to become a more connected, insight-led and efficient organisation, better equipped to meet growing community expectations, increasing service complexity and the need to deliver more value from every dollar. As Data, Integration and Automation Coordinator, you will play a key leadership role in that journey.

This is more than a technical role. It is an opportunity to help shape how Council works into the future by strengthening our data foundations, improving how our systems connect, and unlocking smarter, simpler ways of working across the organisation. You will lead the development of scalable data, integration and automation capability that supports better decision-making, reduces fragmentation and manual effort, and enables more responsive, modern services for our community.

Working across multiple business areas, you will help move Banyule towards a more joined-up and digitally enabled operating environment. Your work will contribute directly to Council's broader transformation agenda by improving information flow, building organisational capability, and embedding solutions that support both immediate service uplift and long-term strategic change.

Why you'll love this role and what you'll do:

As Data, Integration and Automation Coordinator, you will help turn Banyule's transformation ambitions into practical, organisation-wide outcomes. You will work at the intersection of strategy, delivery and enablement, partnering across the business to uplift capability, improve systems and processes, and help Council operate in a more connected and contemporary way.

You will:

Lead Council's data and insight capability by improving the quality, accessibility and governance of data, supporting stronger evidence-based decision-making, service planning and organisational performance.
Drive integration and information flow across Council by connecting systems, reducing duplication and fragmentation, and improving how information moves between platforms, teams and business processes.
Deliver automation and digital efficiency initiatives by identifying opportunities to streamline repetitive work, improve consistency, reduce manual effort and strengthen compliance through smarter use of technology.
Play a key role in organisational transformation by helping embed new ways of working, lifting digital and data maturity, and translating technical capability into meaningful business and community outcomes.

This is a leadership role for someone who can combine strategic thinking with practical delivery. It requires a person who can see the bigger picture, build strong relationships across the organisation, and bring others along through change. You will be part of shaping a more modern Council, one that is better connected internally and better able to serve its community externally.

This role is part of Banyule's Leadership Network; you'll have opportunities to grow your skills and experience of leadership in a Local Government context and develop your skills as an organisational leader beyond your role-based expertise.

What you'll bring/what we are looking for:

We're looking for a strategic, forward-thinking and collaborative professional who can see the bigger picture and turn data, systems and technology into practical organisational improvement. This role will suit someone who enjoys influencing at a strategic level, leading meaningful change, and working across the organisation to strengthen data capability, improve connected systems and drive smarter, more efficient ways of working. You'll bring a strong blend of technical expertise, sound judgement and stakeholder leadership, with the ability to translate complexity into clear direction, practical solutions and measurable outcomes. You'll also bring:

1. A degree in Data Science, Information Systems, Computer Science, Business or a related field, or significant equivalent experience.

2. Demonstrated experience developing and implementing enterprise data strategies, governance frameworks and data policies.

3. Extensive experience delivering data, system integration and automation solutions at scale.

4. Proven ability to lead strategic projects and deliver measurable organisational outcomes.

5. Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to influence, build consensus and work effectively across all levels of the organisation.

6. Advanced understanding of data compliance, privacy and cybersecurity standards.

7. Personal values and behaviours aligned with Banyule City Council's values, with a commitment to collaboration, psychological safety and service excellence.

Pre-employment requirements:

Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check (NCCHC)
Psychometric Assessment

What's on offer

A permanent, full-time role
Salary range $142,754.50 to $153,950.90 per annum (excluding super)
26 weeks of paid parental leave - primary carer/4 weeks of paid parental leave - partner  
Ongoing learning and development opportunities, including health and well-being initiatives
Flexible work and leave arrangements, hybrid work arrangements

Ready to make an impact?

Apply now and shape the way Banyule connects data, systems and technology to deliver smarter services.

How to apply:

Please submit your resume and a cover letter (maximum of three pages) setting out your achievements, skills and experience aligned to the key selection criteria in the position description. Please also respond to the job-specific questions in the application form.

For more information about this position, please view the position description. For a confidential discussion, we encourage you to contact Tim Wakeham on 03 9242 3450.
Applications close: 3 April 2026 at 11:45 PM.

Why choose this opportunity?At Banyule City Council, we value innovation, collaboration, and professional growth. Banyule City Council is an Equal Opportunity Employer; we value diversity and inclusion, and we welcome candidates from all backgrounds. If you have a reasonable adjustment, support, or access requirement, we encourage you to inform us through your application or email employment@banyule.vic.gov.au

Our Values: Our employees align their careers with Banyule because they share our values of respect, integrity, responsibility, initiative, and inclusion. They thrive in our strong learning and development culture and the positive way we work in partnership with the community. You will share our values and have a passion for helping a community of over 130,000 residents to live happier, healthier and more connected lives, all while growing your skills and advancing your career. We look forward to you starting the next chapter of your career at Banyule Council.

Diversity Statement: Our community is made up of diverse cultures, beliefs, abilities, bodies, sexualities, ages, and genders. We are committed to access, equity, participation, and rights for everyone: principles that empower, foster harmony, and increase the well-being of an inclusive community.

To discover more about Banyule's commitment to advancing gender equality in the workplace, please find Banyule's Workplace Gender Equality Action Plan 2021-2025.

Acknowledgment of the Traditional Custodians: Banyule City Council is proud to acknowledge the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as Traditional Custodians of the land, and we pay respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elders, past, present and emerging, who have resided in the area and have been an integral part of the region's history.

Banyule City Council endorses the Uluru Statement from the Heart in full and accepts the invitation to walk with First Nations peoples, to a better future for us all.

Child Safe Standards Statement of Commitment: Banyule City Council is a child-safe organisation committed to the safety and well-being of children.

Council has a zero tolerance for child abuse. All allegations and safety concerns will be treated seriously and acted upon. As a child-safe organisation, we are committed to providing a child-safe environment where children feel safe, are empowered, valued and protected. Council will actively listen to children, ensuring their voices are heard and considered in decisions that affect their lives.

What you will bring
6+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst, ideally across finance operations, procurement and accounting
Strong experience working with ERP platforms, including SAP S/4HANA and/or SAP ECC 6.0 (FICO, procurement-related modules)
Proven capability in end-to-end business process mapping and analysis

Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into business language

Seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join our Design team. 

$190,026 base salary plus superannuation

Permanent FT | Haymarket. Grow your career with us today.  

  

About Ausgrid – we’re wired for good 

We’re the largest distributer of electricity on Australia’s east coast, which means over two million homes and businesses rely on our good work. We’re here at the heart of the energy transition, and we’re driven by a powerful purpose to make electricity accessible for all. 

It’s an exciting new chapter for our company with each day bringing a renewed energy to how we deliver, store and use electricity. As we continue to grow, we’re in search of exceptional talent to help us achieve our goals.  

Everyone’s job is important here. Our customers come from all walks of life, and so do we. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace, and encourage applications from all backgrounds. 

  

See yourself working here 

At Ausgrid, we’re building intelligent, data driven solutions that help power communities across NSW. We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to design, develop and deploy AI and advanced analytics solutions that directly support our business objectives and integrate seamlessly into our enterprise data landscape. You will be based at our Haymarket office and reporting to Manager of Data Services and Platforms.  

This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, working end to end across the AI lifecycle, and partnering closely with the business to deliver real outcomes. 

You’ll collaborate with stakeholders, testers and SMEs, translating business use cases into robust AI solutions that create measurable value. 

This a unique role to work on Work on meaningful AI solutions that support critical infrastructure and communities and work for a business that is at the heart of the energy transition. 

 

Key responsibilities 

AI & Analytics Development: Design, build, train and deploy ML and LLM based models to address specific business use cases, integrating with existing systems and platforms. 

MLOps & Deployment: Leverage Ausgrid’s data platform and established MLOps/LLMOps practices to operationalise models at scale. 

Performance & Monitoring: Implement monitoring, alerting and cost management routines to assess model performance and reliability. 

Data Modelling & Serving: Build Lakehouse aligned data models (Bronze/Silver/Gold), optimising for performance, reliability and cost. 

Security & Governance: Apply strong data security controls, encryption, secrets management and audit logging, in line with governance and regulatory requirements. 

CI/CD & IaC: Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, version control and environment promotion using Azure DevOps/Git and infrastructureascode (Bicep). 

Operations & Support: Monitor production jobs and SLAs, perform root cause analysis and work with support teams to resolve issues. 

Collaboration & Documentation: Engage with stakeholders throughout delivery and produce clear technical documentation, runbooks and data dictionaries. 

 

What you’ll bring  

Our hiring decisions are not only based on your experience and skills, but your passion, attitude, and alignment to our core values.  

 

Essential experience and skills 

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or a related discipline. 

Strong software development experience using Python, Spark and SQL. 

Proven experience working with Azure and largescale, cloud based data workflows. 

Solid database design and optimisation experience (e.g. PostgreSQL). 

Working knowledge of machine learning workflows in Databricks. 

Experience with automated unit and integration testing. 

Familiarity with Agile delivery and MLOps practices. 

Strong full stack mindset with the ability to learn quickly and deliver scalable, high quality solutions. 

  

Life at Ausgrid  

At Ausgrid, we go beyond just a competitive salary to offer an exciting range of benefits. From salary sacrificing electric vehicles and novated leasing, to discounted corporate health insurance and access to Fitness Passport, our benefits support your work here, and your life out there.  

Celebrate success every day with our employee recognition program to bring out each other’s best. Looking to grow professionally? We’ve got you covered with study assistance, secondments, training and mentors, whose advice you’ll keep wherever you go.  We’re proud to offer gender-neutral parental leave to balance work when growing your family. And if you’re caring for elderly parents, our aged care support services offer expert guidance to help you access care for your loved ones. At Ausgrid, it's all about supporting you in every aspect of life.

The SunRice Group is an Australian ASX-listed branded food group, comprising multiple businesses, assets and operations across Australia, New Zealand, the Middle East, the United States, The Pacific Islands and Asia.  SunRice was built by the Australian rice industry over many decades and today is one of Australia’s leading branded food exporters, with approximately 2,000 employees and operations in 11 countries.

SunRice is a great place to work, providing a real opportunity for passionate people to make a difference.

 

About the role

As our Supply Chain Finance Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in ensuring we continue delivering high‑quality, sustainable products while driving value across our ANZ operations.

Sitting at the heart of our supply chain, you’ll provide end‑to‑end finance support across Procurement, Costing and Inventory - enabling smarter purchasing decisions, clear and reliable costing, and continuous improvement across the business. You’ll work closely with Operations Finance, Commercial & Category Finance, and Procurement, turning data into insight and insight into meaningful impact.

You’ll thrive in a fast‑paced environment where resilience, curiosity and problem‑solving are essential. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with large datasets, building models in Excel or Power BI, and influencing decisions across a wide network of stakeholders.

 

 

Key responsibilities will include:

Deliver month‑end reporting across ANZ procurement, co‑manufactured products and grain commodities, including PPV and inventory performance.

Lead forecasting and budgeting cycles for inventory and procurement function

Support costing roll‑ups, standard costing and NPD financial assessments

Own core data areas including standard costs, inventory data integrity, SLOB management

Identify and deliver project savings and optimisation initiatives within the supply chain

Partner with procurement and operational leaders, providing insights that drive commercial decision‑making

Contribute to continuous improvement across systems, processes, and reporting

 

About You

You’re a natural problem‑solver who is curious, collaborative and thrives in a fast‑moving environment. You know how to work with large datasets, untangle complexity, and present insights with clarity and purpose. You enjoy working with people as much as numbers, and you bring resilience and resourcefulness to every challenge.

 

You will bring:

2–4 years’ experience in management accounting, finance or business analysis

Background in supply chain, FMCG or procurement‑related finance (advantageous)

Strong Excel capability and confidence working with large datasets; Power BI experience highly valued

Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify and influence

Experience partnering with teams such as Procurement, Operations Finance, Commercial or Category Finance

SAP experience, ideally in costing modules (preferred)

A degree in Finance, Accounting, Commerce, Economics or Information Systems

 

What’s in it for you

At SunRice Group, we believe great work starts with feeling supported, valued and inspired. When you join our team, you’ll enjoy a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, growth and everyday life.




SunRice Product Allowance: Take home a taste of what we do best. You’ll receive a weekly allowance of SunRice Group products, so you can enjoy and share the food we’re proud to make.

Leave: Enjoy competitive leave benefits, including volunteer leave and the option to purchase additional leave when you need extra flexibility.

Wellness Reimbursement: Your wellbeing matters. Twice a year, you’ll be reimbursed for fitness memberships or other health and wellbeing initiatives that help you feel your best

 

If you enjoy getting into the detail, simplifying complexity, and supporting teams that keep food moving from farms to families, this is a place where your work truly matters.

 

All correspondence will be dealt with in strict confidence.

 

SunRice is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. SunRice will consider qualified applicants for employment based on their merit, individual skills, qualifications and other appropriate criteria relevant to the role for which they are applying.

At Wesfarmers Health we strive to make health, beauty and wellness experiences simpler, more affordable and easier to access for all Australians.

Our portfolio includes well-known names like Priceline, as well as our MediAesthetics brands - Clear Skincare Clinics and SILK Laser Clinics. In the digital space, we're proud to have SISU Health and InstantScripts—and most recently, we've launched our newest retail beauty stores, atomica.





About us:

What began in 1910 as a small co-operative of just three pharmacies has grown into Wesfarmers Health—a leading Australian organisation in health, beauty, and wellness.

Today, Wesfarmers Health proudly employs over 3,600 team members, all committed to delivering accessible, innovative, and trusted health and beauty services to Australians nationwide, across retail, medi-aesthetics, digital health, and wholesale and supply chain operations.

This role combines analytical rigour with commercial thinking and stakeholder engagement. You will work across the full breadth of strategy and transformation, partnering with stakeholders at all levels to solve complex problems and translate strategy into execution.

In this role, you will:

Lead the identification and resolution of key opportunities through structured analysis, synthesis and iteration
Manage smaller independent projects and deliver multiple workstreams within larger strategic initiatives
Develop actionable plans alongside business unit owners to translate strategy into execution
Monitor market trends to identify competitive threats and new business opportunities
Partner with the business development team to evaluate attractive markets and potential targets
Contribute to the annual Corporate Planning process, including industry analysis and business plans for strategic initiatives
Support initiative owners with analysis, business case development and strategy execution

What you will bring:

Minimum of 2-3 years experience in investment banking, strategy consulting or corporate development
A Track record of providing high-quality commercial advice to senior management
The ability to apply critical thinking across both strategy and value creation initiatives
Experience drawing insights from multiple data sources to identify key opportunities
High learning orientation and the agility to work with stakeholders across a broad range of topics

Why us?

Access to employee benefits across Wesfarmers including team member discounts at Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks, OnePass, Priceline, atomica and Clear Skincare Clinics
Ongoing professional development and career opportunities across the Wesfarmers Health Division and the broader Wesfarmers Group
Novated leasing options
Access to our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) - available to all team members and their immediate family members, 24/7, 365 days a year
Beautiful outdoor terrace for work and recreation
Food, coffee and health & wellbeing facilities onsite including a landlord operated gym
Dedicated end of trip facilities (cycle racks, showers, lockers)

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Applicants

Wesfarmers Health aims to become an employer of choice for First Nations Australians, through investments that attract, empower and retain First Nations team members within our stores, distribution centres, clinics and corporate offices.

As part of the Wesfarmers group, we provide authentic support through a diverse range of programs and initiatives, designed to empower you on your journey towards personal achievement and professional advancement.

At Smartways, we specialise in time-critical, complex logistics for the Australasian healthcare community. Our mission is to lift patient outcomes and save lives across all healthcare industries – including medical devices, life sciences, pharmaceuticals, and clinical trials.

As a tech-driven organisation, 95% of our orders are processed online or through seamless system integrations. We leverage AI-optimised routing, real-time GPS tracking, live ETAs, and automated proof-of-delivery – making technology core to every delivery we make.

The Role
The Head of Analytics, AI & Operations is a strategic, senior role responsible for transforming data into intelligence that drives operational excellence across Smartways’ 24/7 healthcare logistics network.

This role goes beyond traditional reporting: the Head of Analytics will harness the power of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and advanced analytics to optimise critical services delivery, improve driver and network efficiency, strengthen customer reporting, and support evidence-based decision-making at every level of the business.

Working at the intersection of data science, AI, and healthcare logistics operations, this leader will build scalable analytics capabilities that directly enhance patient outcomes, reduce operational risk, and support Smartways’ ambitious growth across Australia and New Zealand.

Key Responsibilities:

Analytics & Reporting

Collect, analyse, interpret, and present data from across the business to enable high-quality operational and executive decision-making

Use Power BI, Metabase, and emerging AI-native reporting tools to create visually immersive, interactive, and real-time dashboards for Operations, Customer Service, Finance, and Executive teams

Prepare management reports that clearly articulate trends, patterns, and forward-looking predictions using relevant operational data

Tie together disparate and unrelated data sets – including driver GPS data, POD records, booking system data, temperature logs, and financial data – to produce unified, actionable insights

Perform continuous quality assurance on all data pipelines and reporting, detecting breaks, anomalies, and integrity issues and determining solutions

AI & Advanced Analytics

Design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning models to optimise operations – including predictive routing for time-critical deliveries such as Patient on Table (POT), Direct Drive, and Next Flight services

Build and refine AI-generated routing and dispatch logic that accounts for live traffic conditions, driver availability, and urgency classification to reduce delivery times and improve service reliability

Develop predictive analytics models for demand forecasting, fleet utilisation, and driver capacity planning across all states and New Zealand

Leverage AI to identify operational risk patterns – including late deliveries, temperature excursions, and chain-of-custody gaps – and surface early-warning insights to operations leadership

Evaluate and implement AI tools, large language models (LLMs), and automation capabilities that enhance workflow efficiency across operations, customer service, and dispatch teams

Champion the use of generative AI for internal productivity – including prompt engineering frameworks, AI-assisted report narration, and intelligent alerting systems

Operational Strategy & Stakeholder Engagement

Work alongside Operations, Customer Service, Finance, and IT teams to establish current business intelligence needs and prioritise the analytics roadmap accordingly

Lead cross-state workforce planning initiatives, influencing and governing cross-functional leaders and teams through data-driven insights rather than direct authority

Translate complex analytical findings into clear, practical recommendations that allow operational stakeholders to take immediate, realistic action

Facilitate collaborative planning sessions with stakeholders, aligning subject matter expertise with organisational strategy and data capability

Build trust and credibility with cross-functional stakeholders by embedding data and AI tools into everyday decision-making workflows

Drive accountability and execution by guiding teams from insight to action, ensuring all analytics initiatives are understood, owned, and delivered 

Data Infrastructure & Governance

Make recommendations on data collection methods, system integrations, and analytics infrastructure to improve data quality, coverage, and analytical efficiency

Collaborate with IT to ensure data from Smart Systems (Track & Trace, live GPS, POD, mobile booking, label generation) is structured and accessible for analytics and AI workloads

Maintain data governance standards consistent with ISO 27001 certification requirements and healthcare-sector data security obligations as required

Academic Qualifications

Tertiary qualifications, preferably in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, or a related analytical discipline, with a high level of academic achievement

Postgraduate study, certifications, or specialised training in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or Applied Data Science is highly regarded

Professional certifications in AI/ML platforms (e.g. Microsoft Azure AI, Google Cloud AI, AWS Machine Learning) or data visualisation tools are advantageous

Company description:



Manager, Innovation, Strategy & Delivery (VPS6) | 12‑month backfill

Fixed-term, Full-time | $138,631 - $185,297 + super | 121 Exhibition St, Melbourne + hybrid

Too often, good ideas stall in governance. Promising pilots never scale. Innovation becomes a slide deck rather than a delivery discipline. This role ensures that innovation is governed well, delivered well, and scaled where it matters.

About VIDA

The Victorian Infrastructure Delivery Authority (VIDA) is a specialist project delivery organisation, responsible for delivering the biggest transport and health infrastructure program in Victorian history. This includes more than 200 projects such as the Metro Tunnel, North East Link, West Gate Tunnel, removing level crossings, new and upgraded hospitals in Footscray, Frankston and Warrnambool, and specialist programs such as the early parenting centres expansion and upgrade program.

The unprecedented program of works brings together expert skillsets to deliver crucial health and major transport projects and ensure that operations are streamlined and efficient while delivering better outcomes for Victorians. Led by the Director-General, VIDA is comprised of the four project offices Major Road Projects Victoria, Level Crossing Removal Project, the Metro Tunnel Project, and the Victorian Health Building Authority.

Within VIDA Services, The Hive is VIDA's improvement and innovation capability — helping teams solve complex problems, build innovation capability, pilot practical solutions (including emerging tech), and scale what works across programs for measurable impact.



Job description:

The Manager, Innovation, Strategy and Delivery is a senior strategic leadership role providing enterprise-level oversight and delivery of innovation programs across VIDA. You'll contribute to (and at times lead) strategic planning, enable capability uplift, design supportive frameworks and governance, and lead cross‑agency engagement that helps embed a culture of innovation.

You will:

Design, implement and continuously improve enterprise innovation programs and capability initiatives.
Strengthen innovation governance and alignment with whole‑of‑government and VIDA objectives.
Provide high-level strategic advice on innovation systems, transformation approaches and change enablement; manage strategic risks within public sector governance/probity/financial frameworks.

Lead and support a small team of innovation and technical advisors; produce executive‑level briefings and communications.

About You

You're a senior operator: strategic, credible, and delivery‑minded. Able to work across boundaries and turn ambiguity into forward motion.

You'll bring:

Experience leading innovation or transformation in complex, multi‑stakeholder environments.
Proven ability to build frameworks/systems that uplift capability and deliver measurable value.
Demonstrated, hands‑on experience using AI and emerging technologies in complex organisational environments.
Strong analytical and advisory skills with confidence influencing senior leaders and partners.
Leadership experience building high‑performing, collaborative teams.

Key Selection Criteria

Demonstrated experience in leading innovation or transformation initiatives in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
Proven ability to develop and implement strategic frameworks, systems, or initiatives that enable innovation capability uplift and value delivery.
Advanced conceptual and analytical skills, with a track record of providing authoritative advice to senior leaders and informing strategic decision-making.
Proven ability to manage high-value relationships and influence senior leaders, delivery partners, and external stakeholders.
Demonstrated leadership experience, including managing high-performing teams and fostering a collaborative, values-driven culture

An emphasis on work / life balance
High-performing team environment
Equal opportunity employer

Committed to safety & wellbeing

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Commercial Performance and Governance team as a Senior Finance Business Partner – Commercial Excellence!

 

Stay and grow with us!

14% superannuation (for EA team member) is standard here - because your future matters.

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As the Senior Finance Business Partner – Commercial Excellence, you will play a pivotal role in driving the organisation’s commercial management uplift across the enterprise, collaborating closely with key stakeholders to enhance Performance and Partnering Operations. You will work with internal and external stakeholders to deliver the Commercial Excellence program through shaping business initiatives, conducting in depth analysis and providing recommendations, to drive value.

Professional accounting qualifications such as Institute of Chartered Accountants (CA ANZ), Certified Practising Accounts Australia (CPA) or equivalent.
Proven experience in a role that provides critical and actionable expenditure insights into financial performance.
Strong understanding of cost allocation processes and behaviours such processes drive.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely, and influence at senior/executive management level.
Proven experience in the preparation and presentation of financial and non-financial reports for senior/executive stakeholders.

Advanced level Excel and Word skills.
