Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Friday, 3 April 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

A bit about us:

At Interactive, our customers trust us with their most important data, critical devices and security. We’re Australian owned, 500+ strong, and built for high-performance managed services.

Our Cloud services span 24/7 managed services, migrations and consulting across multiple public clouds plus our own private cloud.

The role:

As our Head of Cloud Procurement, you’ll own the commercial engine behind our Cloud portfolio - governance, vendor strategy, pricing inputs and cost optimisation. You’ll relentlessly hunt cloud cost-reduction opportunities, sharpen our data and processes, and partner closely with Sales, Presales and Product to land deals, protect margin and drive sustainable growth.

Some of the key things you'll do:

Be the commercial connector across presales, delivery, finance and procurement - aligning outcomes end to end.
Own and optimise the cost base for cloud services - driving efficiency without compromising delivery.
Lift Cloud budgeting, forecasting and margin management - building cadence, accountability and visibility.
Chair the Pricing Review Board - set guardrails, test assumptions, and ensure pricing is commercially right.
Lead deal reviews with commercial insight, risk appetite and sharp financial modelling.

What we’re looking for:

Proven experience procuring Cloud and aligned services (ideally in an MSP or cloud-native environment).
Strong knowledge of the IT vendor and services landscape - commercial models, levers and negotiation angles.
Deep capability in cost-stack analysis across complex cloud environments.
Strong financial acumen - able to translate complex cost structures into clear commercial decisions and outcomes.
Executive-level stakeholder management - credible, persuasive and comfortable driving change.

Ready to make Cloud procurement a competitive advantage? Apply now, we’d love to meet you.

Professional leadership & people management

Lead defect triage for complex issues and provide Go / No‑Go recommendations based on risk
Essential criteria

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Extensive experience in Business Analysis and project delivery, with 3–5+ years in a leadership or management role
Proven people leadership capability, including mentoring, performance management and capability uplift

Confidence working across Waterfall, Agile and Hybrid environments, applying the right approach based on risk
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to influence at all levels
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and documentation skills, with high attention to detail
Comfort working with project and delivery tools, such as Smartsheet or similar platforms

Job Title: EOI Finance Professionals at Trilogy Care
Ad Text:
Trilogy Care is one of Australia's fastest-growing health and aged care providers, and we're building the finance function to match. Not a traditional back-office. A modern financial operations team where accountants write SQL alongside journal entries, where Python replaces copy-paste, and where AI agents handle the work that used to eat your week.

We're opening expressions of interest from finance professionals who have the technical rigour of a CPA/CA but think like systems builders. If you've ever looked at a month-end close and thought "I could automate this" - we want to talk to you.

Who we're looking for

We're connecting with finance and accounting professionals at all levels - junior, mid-level, and senior accountants, financial analysts and emerging finance leaders ready for their next step.

Across management accounting, financial reporting, commercial finance, and operations - we have roles available now with more to come as the team scales.

What makes you a fit

You don't wait for a manual. You write it in Claude Code and then automate it.

Open to AI: You have an open mind to learn and apply the latest in AI thinking. You're not waiting for permission — you're already experimenting with LLMs, copilots, and automation in your day-to-day work, and you want an employer that encourages it.
Systems thinker: You look at how things work end-to-end, spot what's broken or inefficient, and design them to be better. You think in workflows and processes, not just transactions.
Comfortable in ambiguity: High-growth companies don't have a playbook for everything. You find that energising, not stressful.
Tech-first: Excel is your baseline, not your ceiling. You're already using (or learning) SQL, Power BI, or Python to move faster.

Why Trilogy Care

Trilogy Care is a national health and aged care provider on a high-growth trajectory with a strong purpose and an evolving future. Our finance team plays a critical role in enabling strategic decision-making, supporting sustainable growth, and helping the business continue to make a meaningful difference.

This is an opportunity to join a business that values:

Strong attitude and capability
Innovation and continuous improvement
Technology and AI as core tools, not afterthoughts
Growth, ownership, and impact

If you’re a finance professional who speaks the language of both data and business, and you’re looking to be part of a progressive, high-growth organisation building a modern finance function - we’d love to hear from you.

Interested?

Submit your expression of interest with your CV. Tell us what excites you about modern finance - bonus points if you can show us something you've built or automated. Send in what you’ve made or a link to your GitHub repo as part of the application process.

• Collaborate with stakeholders to enhance critical employee interactions across the employee lifecycle, ensuring alignment with organisational objectives and to enhance employee satisfaction.

• Support the delivery of projects aimed at enhancing People and Culture processes, ensuring timely and successful delivery, escalating where appropriate.

• Mentor and guide other members of the People and Culture department to ensure that initiatives are considering user experience.

Role: FP&A Lead

Length: 6-months with a view to extend/perm

Location: Melbourne / Hybrid

Sector: Private-equity backed Professional Services

Reports to: CFO

The Opportunity

Our client is a prominent, private-equity backed professional services group with a significant footprint across APAC. As the business continues to modernise its financial operations, they are seeking an ambitious Senior FP&A Lead to join as their first dedicated FP&A hire.

This is a "greenfield" opportunity. Rather than inheriting a legacy system, you will have the autonomy to architect the financial planning framework for a complex, multi-brand organisation. You will be the primary driver behind professionalising how the Group sets its budgets, forecasts, and how it evaluates its commercial drivers.

The Role

This is a high-visibility position designed for a "builder." Your objective is to move the finance function from reactive reporting to proactive, high-value strategic partnership.

Key Responsibilities:

Greenfield Model Development: Build the Group's foundational budget and rolling forecast models from scratch, ensuring they are robust, scalable, and user-friendly.
Revenue Logic & Integration: Develop complex models that link operational billing metrics to revenue recognition standards, providing clarity on the firm's primary income streams.
Process Transformation: Audit existing data workflows and implement standardised FP&A processes across different business units to improve data integrity and reporting speed.
Commercial Insight: Provide the Executive team with "what-if" scenario modeling and deep-dive analysis into margin performance and headcount productivity.

Technical Profile

FP&A Specialist: 5+ years of experience in a dedicated FP&A or Commercial Finance role, ideally within a professional services environment
Master Modeler: You possess advanced Excel skills and a proven track record of building integrated financial models (P&L, Cash Flow, Balance Sheet) from the ground up.
Analytical Rigor: You have experience navigating high-volume billing data and can translate technical accounting requirements into commercial reporting.
Change Agent: You are comfortable working in an environment of transition and have the communication skills to influence stakeholders during a period of process improvement.
Systems Mindset: Experience with Power BI or EPM software (e.g., Anaplan, Workday Adaptive, or Jedox) is highly desirable.

Why Join?

This role offers a unique career milestone: the chance to define the FP&A function for a listed entity. You will have a direct line to the CFO and the opportunity to see your models become the standard for the entire Group.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Lending Transformation (12-Month FTC)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support a large, strategic lending project focused on delivering an uplift to our home loan and lending capability. 

This is a 12-month max-term contract, with the option to extend based on project needs and performance. 

Job Description


This is a hands-on role, focused on requirements, process design, data and operating model change in a fast-paced environment.

Lead end-to-end business analysis for complex lending initiatives, from discovery through to implementation and review.
Work across residential mortgage lending, platform and system change, core banking and servicing, including end-to-end mortgage operations.
Support transformation and migration activities, including data and process migration across platforms and providers.
Partner with technology, operations, credit, risk and compliance to deliver scalable, compliant solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Business Analysis & Requirements

Lead discovery, stakeholder workshops and define clear business requirements, user stories and acceptance criteria.
Translate complex needs into actionable artefacts for engineering, operations and vendors.

Process & Journey Design

Map current and future-state processes across origination, servicing and collections.
